constructor: Venkat Porto (drafted by Claude Opus stand-in)
purpose: Seven per-factor weight-perturbation variants for O'Brien, the N.J. 1983 Wade-factor adoption case. Second Wade-factor instance in the pilot (first was Barker). Tests whether the Wade/Barker small-C0/C3-delta and F3-alternative-precautions collapse pattern replicates on a different Wade case.
construction_notes: O'Brien is a 4-3 split case, which means ground-truth unanimity is absent. It is retained in the pilot as a non-primary case per SPEC.md §5 constraint 8. The split specifically turned on factor 3 (availability of safer substitute), which is the predicted collapse factor for Wade cases. This makes O'Brien a natural stress-test of whether the model sides with the majority or the dissent when the dispositive factor is perturbed.
All 7 Wade factors present — this is a full-Wade case unlike Barker which used Barker's 5-factor variant.

Two anti-liability factors (f1 product_utility, f6 user_awareness) — more counterweight than Barker's one-factor-at-f4 plus f5. O'Brien is therefore a better test of the 'perturb the counterweight' measurement.

perturbations.original_text: The usefulness and desirability of above-ground swimming pools to the user and public — recreation, exercise, affordability relative to in-ground pools.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether above-ground swimming pools provide substantial utility to consumers and the public is treated by the court as neither clearly high nor clearly low; the utility question is not resolved in either direction in the record.
perturbations.perturbation_note: First of two anti-liability factors. Perturbing f1 removes one counterweight.

perturbations.original_text: The likelihood of serious injury from foreseeable uses of the pool, including diving into shallow water.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether the likelihood of injury from foreseeable uses including diving is substantial is not developed as a clearly load-bearing element in the court's analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Tied-highest pro-liability factor. A model integrating should shift.

perturbations.original_text: The probable seriousness of diving-injury harm — spinal cord damage, paralysis, death.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: The probable seriousness of diving-injury harm is treated by the court as one consideration among several, not developed as a dispositive factor in the risk-utility balance.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Tied-highest pro-liability factor. Pair-testing with f2 perturbation measures whether the model treats probability and gravity as a unified 'safety aspects' block per the Wade 2 split.

perturbations.original_text: The availability of a safer substitute — in-ground pools, deeper above-ground designs, textured-bottom materials, or warning/label redesign.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether a safer substitute product exists that would meaningfully reduce the diving-injury risk is contested in the record; neither the presence nor absence of such a substitute is resolved by the court's analysis as presented here.
perturbations.perturbation_note: This is the 4-3 split factor. In O'Brien itself, the majority and dissent disagreed on this exact question. Perturbing it is the critical collapse test: if Wade collapse targets F3 (alternative_precautions) as predicted from Barker, this perturbation should produce the biggest shift in the direct probe.

perturbations.perturbed_wade_factor: Wade 4 (ability to eliminate unsafe character without unreasonable cost)
perturbations.original_text: The manufacturer's ability to eliminate or mitigate the diving-injury risk without impairing the pool's utility or imposing unreasonable cost — additional warnings, bottom-texture changes, design modifications.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether the manufacturer could have eliminated or mitigated the risk without unreasonable cost or utility impairment is not developed as a dispositive element; the court treats this factor as balanced.

perturbations.original_text: The user's anticipated awareness of the danger of diving into a four-foot pool — arguably obvious to adults but contested for younger or inexperienced users.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: The anticipated user awareness of the diving-injury risk is not clearly established on the record as either high or low; the court treats this factor as balanced between the obviousness argument and the counter-argument that spinal injury risks are not widely understood.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Second of two anti-liability factors. Perturbing f1 AND f6 together would remove both counterweights, but single perturbations test each in isolation.

perturbations.original_text: The feasibility of spreading the loss by raising the product's price or by the manufacturer carrying insurance, as compared with placing the loss on the injured user.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: The feasibility of spreading the loss through price or insurance rather than placing it on the injured user is treated by the court as a consideration worth noting but not as a load-bearing element.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Lowest-weight factor (0.10). Near-stub. Perturbation is near-no-op.

reconstruction_check_prompt: Does the case description above omit any factor you believe a court would typically weigh in this analysis? If yes, name the omitted factor and explain what weight you would assign it. If no, say so.
open_items_for_pilot: The key measurement is f4 (alternative_precautions) — this is the 4-3 split factor and the predicted Wade collapse target per the Barker result. If the direct probe shifts dramatically when f4 is perturbed, the doctrine-aware-collapse hypothesis is replicated.
